issues: silhouette too thin and elongated at 1x — nearly unreadable as a sword without context
no roundness or softness in shape — blade is sharp/edgy which conflicts with Echoes of Wisdom toy-charm aesthetic
guard/crossguard is tiny and blends into the blade at 1x
orange accent pixels on hilt are charming but insufficient to rescue overall coldness
contrast is acceptable on dark bg but the thin diagonal silhouette reads as a line/stick not a sword at 1x
reasoning: The pixel art sword has decent contrast on both dark and light backgrounds at 4x, and the cyan/teal blade color with orange hilt accents shows some personality. However it fails the sticker test at 1x — the silhouette is too thin and diagonal to be instantly readable as a sword; it reads as a line or scratch. The shape is angular and aggressive rather than round/soft/toylike as required by the Echoes of Wisdom charm reference. There is no chunky friendliness: the blade tapers to a sharp point, the guard is minimal, and the overall impression is more generic RPG weapon than cute memorable icon. Clear-but-charmless at 4x, mushy-and-unreadable at 1x — lands in marginal territory.
